notes: All content can be assessed on any of the three question papers. Questions may draw together elements from different topic areas. Foundation tier covers grades 1-5, Higher tier covers grades 4-9.

papers.name: Paper 1
papers.description: Non-calculator paper. Content from any part of the specification may be assessed.

papers.name: Paper 2
papers.description: Calculator paper. Content from any part of the specification may be assessed.

papers.name: Paper 3
papers.description: Calculator paper. Content from any part of the specification may be assessed.

subtopics.name: Structure and calculation - order integers, decimals, fractions; use symbols =, ≠, <, >, ≤, ≥

Structure and calculation - inverse operations, priority of operations, brackets, powers, roots, reciprocals

Structure and calculation - positive integer powers, roots, index laws
Structure and calculation - fractional indices (Higher only)
Structure and calculation - surds (Higher only)

Fractions, decimals and percentages - standard form
Fractions, decimals and percentages - fractions and reciprocals of numbers
Fractions, decimals and percentages - terminating decimals and fractions
Fractions, decimals and percentages - recurring decimals (Higher only)
Measures and accuracy - rounding, estimation, bounds (Higher only)
Measures and accuracy - upper and lower bounds (Higher only)
Fractions, decimals and percentages - percentage change, reverse percentages
Notation, vocabulary and manipulation - algebraic notation
Notation, vocabulary and manipulation - substitute values into expressions and formulae
Notation, vocabulary and manipulation - simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions
Notation, vocabulary and manipulation - collect like terms, multiply single term, take out common factors
Notation, vocabulary and manipulation - rearrange formulae
Graphs - coordinates, plot graphs, identify key points
Graphs - interpret graphs of linear and quadratic functions
Graphs - work with equations of straight lines
Graphs - plot and interpret graphs including reciprocal, exponential, circle (Higher only)
Graphs - equation of a circle (Higher only)
Graphs - find approximate solutions using a graph
Solving equations and inequalities - linear equations
Solving equations and inequalities - linear inequalities
Solving equations and inequalities - quadratic equations
Solving equations and inequalities - simultaneous equations
Solving equations and inequalities - iteration (Higher only)
Sequences - generate terms, nth term of linear sequences
Sequences - nth term of quadratic sequences (Higher only)
Sequences - simple geometric progressions (Higher only)
Sequences - Fibonacci sequences (Higher only)
Ratio, proportion and rates of change - direct and inverse proportion (algebraic)
Algebraic fractions - simplify, add, subtract, multiply, divide (Higher only)
Algebraic proof - use algebra to support arguments (Higher only)
Functions - function notation, composite functions, inverse functions (Higher only)
Graphs - trigonometric functions and their graphs (Higher only)

topics.name: Ratio, Proportion and Rates of Change

subtopics.name: Change between standard units
Use scale factors, scale diagrams and maps
Express one quantity as a fraction of another
Use ratio notation, divide a quantity in a given ratio
Understand proportion, direct and inverse proportion
Express a multiplicative relationship between quantities as a ratio or fraction
Understand and use proportion as equality of ratios
Relate ratios to fractions and linear functions
Define percentage as number of parts per hundred, interpret percentages and changes
Solve problems involving percentage change, original value, simple and compound interest
Solve problems involving direct and inverse proportion including algebraic (Higher only)
Compare lengths, areas and volumes using ratio notation (Higher only)
Understand X is inversely proportional to Y as equivalent to X proportional to 1/Y (Higher only)
Interpret equations describing direct and inverse proportion (Higher only)
Interpret the gradient at a point on a curve as instantaneous rate of change (Higher only)
Construct and interpret growth and decay graphs including exponential (Higher only)
Set up, solve and interpret answers in growth and decay problems including compound interest (Higher only)
Properties and constructions - use conventional terms and notation
Properties and constructions - ruler and compass constructions
Properties and constructions - properties of angles at a point, on a line, vertically opposite
Properties and constructions - alternate, corresponding angles, interior angles
Properties and constructions - angle sum of triangles, quadrilaterals
Properties and constructions - properties of special quadrilaterals and triangles
Properties and constructions - congruence criteria for triangles
Properties and constructions - geometrical problems using properties of angles
Properties and constructions - identify, describe and construct congruent and similar shapes
Properties and constructions - describe changes and invariance after transformations
Properties and constructions - circle theorems (Higher only)
Properties and constructions - solve geometrical problems on coordinate axes
Mensuration and calculation - standard units of measure, compound units
Mensuration and calculation - perimeter and area of standard shapes
Mensuration and calculation - calculate arc lengths, angles and areas of sectors (Higher only)
Mensuration and calculation - surface area and volume of prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones, spheres
Mensuration and calculation - calculate surface areas and volumes of composite solids (Higher only)
Mensuration and calculation - understand and use vector notation, parallel vectors
Mensuration and calculation - apply addition and subtraction of vectors (Higher only)
Mensuration and calculation - use vectors to construct geometric arguments and proofs (Higher only)
Properties and constructions - apply angle facts, triangle congruence, similarity (Higher only)
Properties and constructions - know exact values of sin, cos, tan at key angles (Higher only)
Properties and constructions - apply Pythagoras and trigonometry in 2D and 3D (Higher only)
Properties and constructions - know exact values of sin, cos, tan for 30, 60, 90 degrees
Record, describe and analyse the frequency of outcomes of probability experiments
Understand probability conventions and probability scales
Relate relative expected frequencies to theoretical probability
Apply the property that probabilities sum to 1
Understand exhaustive sets, mutually exclusive events
Enumerate sets and combinations systematically using tables, grids, Venn diagrams and tree diagrams
Construct theoretical possibility spaces for single and combined experiments
Calculate the probability of independent and dependent combined events
Calculate and interpret conditional probabilities (Higher only)
Understand and use set notation to describe events (Higher only)
Interpret and construct tables, charts and diagrams for categorical and numerical data
Interpret and construct pie charts, stem and leaf diagrams
Interpret and construct bar charts, frequency polygons
Interpret, analyse and compare distributions of data sets
Apply statistics to describe a population
Use and interpret scatter graphs, recognise correlation
Understand sampling methods, random sampling, capture-recapture (Higher only)
Interpret, analyse and compare distributions including measures of central tendency and spread
Construct and interpret cumulative frequency diagrams, box plots (Higher only)
Construct and interpret histograms including unequal class intervals (Higher only)
